title: pseudolysimachion spicatum subsp. orchideum
intro: Pseudolysimachion spicatum subsp. orchideum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the white mustard or white charlock. It is a weed that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Pseudolysimachion spicatum subsp. orchideum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
